content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

Find a pair of indices (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that:


1≤i,j≤N,i≠j1 \le i, j \le N, i \ne j1≤i,j≤N,i=j;
Ai+AjA_i + A_jAi​+Aj​ is not prime†^{\dagger}†.
In case multiple (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) pairs are valid, you can print any one. If it is impossible to find such (i,j)(i, j)(i,j), output −1-1−1 instead.

†^{\dagger}† A number x (x≥2)x \ (x \ge 2)x (x≥2) is said to be prime if it has exactly 222 positive divisors - 111, and xxx itself.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output:



Two space-separated integers iii and jjj, denoting a valid pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j).
−1-1−1 if there exist no valid pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j).

In case multiple (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) pairs are valid, you can print any one.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 : A1+A2=9A_1 + A_2 = 9A1​+A2​=9 which is not prime .
Test case 222 : A1+A3=4A_1 + A_3 = 4A1​+A3​=4 which is not prime. (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4) is also a valid answer.
Test case 333 : All values of Ai+AjA_i + A_jAi​+Aj​ for (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) satisfying i≠ji \ne ji=j are either 222 or 333, both of which are prime. Hence, the answer is −1-1−1.
